title: rhodax distachyos subsp. stipulatus
intro: Rhodax distachyos subsp. stipulatus, also known as the 'Bitter Apple', is a plant species within the Capparaceae family, native to the Mediterranean region. The plant is used in traditional medicine for its bitter and purgative properties, which have been known to stimulate digestion and relieve symptoms of indigestion and constipation. The medicinal properties of Rhodax distachyos subsp. stipulatus are attributed to its ability to stimulate bile production, improve appetite, and reduce inflammation in the digestive tract. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be taken internally to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of digestive disorders.
